sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: West Ham United have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, securing 3 wins, 1 draw, and 1 loss, with a strong tendency to score first and perform well in the first half. Manchester United, on the other hand, are in excellent form with 4 wins in their last 5 matches, displaying late-game strength with a high percentage of goals in the 76-90 minute period.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head History
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Manchester United have a slight edge with 1 win and 1 draw against West Ham. The most recent match on December 4, 2025, ended in a 1-1 draw, highlighting a competitive balance with both teams scoring.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Manchester United sit 5th in the league with 44 points, boasting a strong attack (46 goals scored) and a balanced defense. West Ham, struggling at 18th with 23 points, have conceded 48 goals (second-worst in the league) and lack consistency in possession and chance creation.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: For West Ham, Crysencio Summerville has been in top form, averaging an 8.22 Sofascore rating over the last 5 matches with 5 goals. Manchester United’s Bruno Fernandes remains pivotal, leading the league in assists (12) and key passes, with a 7.68 average rating recently.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: West Ham’s strength in ground duels (league rank 2) could challenge United’s midfield, but their defensive frailties may be exploited by United’s late-game scoring ability. Manchester United’s higher possession and final third entries suggest they’ll dominate play at London Stadium.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Manchester United’s superior form, league standing, and attacking prowess, coupled with West Ham’s defensive struggles, the Sofascore prediction of a 61% away win probability aligns with the data. However, West Ham’s early scoring threat could make it a close contest.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between West Ham United and Manchester United at the London Stadium ended in a goalless draw. Neither side managed to find the back of the net, with both teams showing moments of promise but lacking the cutting edge in the final third. Manchester United dominated possession and showed more intent in attack, while West Ham struggled to create clear-cut opportunities despite some periods of control.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: Manchester United were the more dominant side in terms of ball possession and territorial advantage, spending more time in the final third and registering more entries into the penalty area. Their physicality was evident as they won a significant majority of duels, particularly in the air, showcasing their ability to control the game’s tempo. West Ham, on the other hand, were less effective in duels and struggled to maintain consistent pressure, though they managed to carve out a few moments of threat. Defensively, both teams were relatively solid, with neither conceding significant chances, but Manchester United’s attacking metrics suggest they were closer to breaking the deadlock.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum breakdown
halftimeSections.text: The game started with Manchester United taking early control, dictating the pace in the opening minutes with consistent attacking spells. West Ham responded with a shift in momentum around the midway point of the half, enjoying a prolonged period of pressure and creating some dangerous moments. However, Manchester United regained the upper hand as the half progressed, launching several sustained attacks and maintaining dominance for the majority of the latter stages. West Ham had a brief resurgence towards the end of the half, but it wasn’t enough to tilt the balance significantly in their favor.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For West Ham, Jarrod Bowen remains a potential game-changer with his ability to exploit spaces in the opposition defense, while Crysencio Summerville showed glimpses of creativity in the first half. On the Manchester United side, Luke Shaw stood out with a notable attacking contribution from the back, and Bruno Fernandes’ vision in midfield could be crucial in unlocking West Ham’s defense. Both teams will likely rely on these players to make the difference in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical outlook for second half
halftimeSections.text: Manchester United are likely to continue their high-possession approach, looking to capitalize on their physical dominance and create more opportunities in the penalty area. They may push for an early goal to gain momentum. West Ham, meanwhile, will need to improve their duel-winning ability and focus on counter-attacks to exploit any gaps left by Manchester United’s forward push. Defensive discipline will be key for the Hammers if they are to hold firm and turn the game in their favor.
